zh.lifeSummary: 當Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne於1255年出生在法國迪瑟 德 可勒爾蒙特時，他的父親Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE30歲，母親Yolande26歲。他於1275年1月10日與Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1296年11月24日享年41歲，埋葬在法國奧維普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne于1255年出生在法国迪瑟 德 可勒爾蒙特时，他的父亲Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE30岁，母亲Yolande26岁。他于1275年1月10日与Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1296年11月24日享年41岁，埋葬在法国奧維普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Tour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Middle English tor(re) ‘rocky peak, hill’ (Old English torr). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on or by a hill, or toponymic, from a place so named, such as Torre, the earlier name of Torquay (Devon). The element is especially common in placenames in southwestern England, particularly Devon, but it is occasionally found in other English regions, such as Sussex and Derbyshire.
nickname for someone thought to resemble a bull, from Old French tor (from Latin taurus).

nameMeanings.name: Bernard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from ber(n) ‘bear’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. This was the name of three famous medieval churchmen: St Bernard of Menthon ( 923–1008 ), founder of a hospice on each of the Alpine passes named after him; the monastic reformer St Bernard of Clairvaux ( 1090–1153 ); and the scholastic philosopher Bernard of Chartres. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England. A native Old English form, Beornheard, was superseded by the Norman form.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne was born in 1255, in Diocese of Clermont, France, his father, Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE, was 30 and his mother, Yolande, was 26. He married Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues on 10 January 1275, in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 24 November 1296, at the age of 41, and was buried in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne est né en 1255, à Diocèse de Clermont, France, son père, Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Yolande, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues le 10 janvier 1275, à Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 24 novembre 1296, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne 1255 in Diocèse de Clermont, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE 30 und seine Mutter Yolande 26. Er hat am 10. Januar 1275 in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 24. November 1296 im Alter von 41 Jahren und wurde in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne è nato il nel 1255 nel Diocèse de Clermont, Francia, il suo padre, Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Yolande, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues il 10 gennaio 1275, nel Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 24 novembre 1296, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolto a Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergneが1255年にフランスディオクeゼ ド クラーモントで生まれた時，父Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNEは30歳，母Yolandeは26歳であった。1275年1月10日にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメクラーモント-フエランドでBéatrix de Meymont d’Olierguesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1296年11月24日に41歳で死去し，フランスアウバーグンプウイ-デ-ドoメクラーモント-フエランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne(이)가 1255년 프랑스 디오ce스 데 클레몽에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Yolande(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1275년 1월 10일 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 클레몽-페랜드에서 Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1296년 11월 24일 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 오버그니 푸이-데-드o 클레몽-페랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne nasceu em 1255, em Diocèse de Clermont, França, seu pai, Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Yolande, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues em 10 de janeiro de 1275, em Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de novembro de 1296, com 41 anos, e foi sepultado em Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne родился в 1255 г. в местности диокeс де клирмонт, Франция, его отцу по имени Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Yolande, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues 10 января 1275 г. в местности клирмонт-фирранд, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 24 ноября 1296 г. в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности клирмонт-фирранд, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bernard II la Tour d'Auvergne nació en 1255, en Diocèse de Clermont, Francia, su papá, Bernard IV de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Yolande, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Béatrix de Meymont d’Oliergues el 10 de enero de 1275, en Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 24 de noviembre de 1296, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultado en Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia.
